questions.question: Prove that the angle in a semicircle is a right angle.
questions.answer: Using the properties of a triangle formed by the diameter as hypotenuse, the angle opposite the diameter is 90°.

questions.question: Prove that angles subtended by the same chord at the circumference are equal.
questions.answer: Using isosceles triangles formed by radii to chord endpoints, it can be shown that angles at the circumference are equal.
answer_outline: Select points C and D on same side

questions.question: Prove that equal chords of a circle subtend equal angles at the center.
questions.answer: Draw triangles formed by radii to endpoints of chords and use congruence to show central angles are equal.
answer_outline: Draw equal chords AB and CD

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular from the center to a chord bisects the chord.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by radii and chord segments are congruent, so the perpendicular divides the chord equally.
answer_outline: Draw perpendicular OM from center O

questions.question: Prove that the line joining the midpoints of two parallel chords passes through the center of the circle.
questions.answer: Midpoints lie on a line perpendicular to chords and intersect at the center.
answer_outline: Draw parallel chords AB and CD
Show perpendicular to chords passes through center O

questions.question: Prove that the sum of the opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is 180°.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem to show sum of opposite angles equals 180°.
answer_outline: Identify opposite angles A and C

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is cyclic if a pair of its opposite angles sum to 180°.
questions.answer: Using the converse of the inscribed angle theorem, quadrilateral is cyclic.

questions.question: Prove that the tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal in length.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by radii and tangents are congruent, proving equal lengths.
answer_outline: Draw tangents PA and PB from external point P

questions.question: Prove that the angle between tangent and chord equals the angle in the alternate segment.
questions.answer: By constructing radius and triangle, angle equality is established.
answer_outline: Draw chord AB and tangent at A
Identify angle between tangent and chord
Conclude equal to angle in alternate segment

questions.question: Prove that the radius drawn to the point of contact of a tangent is perpendicular to the tangent.
questions.answer: Using property of shortest distance from center to line, radius meets tangent at 90°.
answer_outline: Draw radius to point of contact

questions.question: Prove that the sum of angles subtended by two chords intersecting inside a circle is 180°.
questions.answer: Use properties of cyclic quadrilateral formed by intersection and chord endpoints.
answer_outline: Draw intersecting chords AB and CD at P

questions.question: Prove that the product of segments of two intersecting chords is equal.
questions.answer: Using similarity of triangles formed by intersecting chords.
answer_outline: Draw intersecting chords AB and CD at P
Identify segments PA, PB, PC, PD

questions.question: Prove that tangents at ends of a diameter are parallel.
questions.answer: Tangents at diameter endpoints form right angles with radii, hence are parallel.
answer_outline: Draw tangents at A and B

questions.question: Prove that angles in the same segment of a circle are equal.
questions.answer: Use isosceles triangles with radii and chord endpoints to establish equality of angles.
answer_outline: Identify points C and D on same segment

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular bisector of a chord passes through the center.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by radii and chord segments are congruent, so perpendicular bisector passes through center.

questions.question: Prove that a line joining midpoints of two equal chords passes through the center.
questions.answer: Use symmetry and perpendicular bisector property to show line passes through center.
answer_outline: Draw equal chords AB and CD

questions.question: Prove that equal chords are equidistant from the center.
questions.answer: Using perpendiculars from center to chords and congruent triangles, distances are equal.
answer_outline: Draw equal chords AB and CD

questions.question: Prove that the external angle formed by two tangents equals the angle subtended by the line joining points of contact at the center.
questions.answer: Construct triangle with center and points of contact to show angle relation.
answer_outline: Draw tangents and points of contact
Show external angle equals central subtended angle

questions.question: Prove that the line joining centers of two touching circles passes through the point of contact.
questions.answer: By definition of touching circles, distance between centers equals sum/difference of radii, passing through contact point.
answer_outline: Identify centers and point of contact
Conclude line passes through contact point

questions.question: Prove that if two tangents intersect at an external point, the lengths from the point to points of contact are equal.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by tangents and radii are congruent, proving equal lengths.

questions.question: Prove that the angle subtended by a chord at the center is greater than the angle subtended on the remaining part of the circle.
questions.answer: Using triangle with radius and chord, central angle > inscribed angle.

questions.question: Prove that the tangents at the ends of a diameter are parallel.
questions.answer: Tangents at diameter endpoints are perpendicular to radii, so parallel to each other.
answer_outline: Draw tangents at A and B

questions.question: Prove that the product of the lengths of segments of two intersecting chords is equal.
questions.answer: Use similarity of triangles formed by intersecting chords to show equality of products.

questions.question: Prove that angles in the same segment of a circle are equal.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem and properties of isosceles triangles to show equality.

questions.question: Prove that the radius drawn to the point of contact of a tangent is perpendicular to the tangent.
questions.answer: Using shortest distance from center to tangent, radius meets tangent at 90°.
answer_outline: Draw radius to point of contact
